problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Glial cell activation/suppression in target brain region
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Glial. Glial cell activation/suppression in target brain region directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Longitudinal patient cohort with 17 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The important thing is not to stop questioning. — Einstein
